prompt: Add TTL expiration to the in-memory cache

turns.content:
<task>
Add TTL (time-to-live) expiration support to the cache. Entries should automatically expire after the specified TTL.
</task>

1	from typing import Any, Optional, Dict
   2	
   3	class Cache:
   4	    def __init__(self):
   5	        self._store: Dict[str, Any] = {}
   6	
   7	    def get(self, key: str) -> Optional[Any]:
   8	        return self._store.get(key)
   9	
  10	    def set(self, key: str, value: Any):
  11	        self._store[key] = value
  12	
  13	    def delete(self, key: str) -> bool:
  14	        if key in self._store:
  15	            del self._store[key]
  16	            return True
  17	        return False
  18	
  19	    def size(self) -> int:
  20	        return len(self._store)

arguments.summary: Read cachelib/cache.py (20 lines). Cache class with dict-backed get/set/delete/size. No TTL support — values stored raw. Need to store (value, expiry_time) tuples and check expiry in get().

next_steps.description: Modify Cache to store (value, expires_at) tuples with optional TTL parameter on set()

arguments.summary: Added TTL support to cachelib/cache.py. Store changed from Dict[str,Any] to Dict[str, Tuple[Any, Optional[float]]] with expiry timestamp. get() checks expiry and auto-deletes expired entries. set() accepts optional ttl in seconds. size() cleans expired keys. All 6 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Added TTL expiration to Cache in cachelib/cache.py. set() accepts optional ttl parameter, get() auto-expires entries, size() cleans expired keys. All 6 tests pass.
